items.title: Good and Bad
items.summary: Let's talk about good and bad. Not in an alignment sense,…
items.content_html:
Let's talk about good and bad.

Not in an alignment sense, but sort of.

I mean quality. Ability. Competence.

These things are easy to see in professionals like doctors or lawyers.

A good lawyer can save your life.

A good doctor can stop you from being sewed.

Something like that.

But a bad one?

Woo boy.

The thing that I learned is that bad professionals can give great results.

A broken clock, after all.

And the other way.

Good ones can fail.

It's not a foolproof thing, because there are humans involved.

And here's the thing I wrestle with.

Would I rather have a bad lawyer or doctor who produced (through no ability of their own) a great result?

Or, a good one who sheet the proverbial bed?

I know the right way to think is the latter.

You want the latter.

The good on a bad day.

The good always.

But results driven people like me ponder.

Hesitate.

Great results are all that matter.

Right?

Right???

Perhaps I am too particular.

A poorly written cover letter just sours me.

As does a lack of charm.

These are perhaps not the best way to gauge things, but alas, here we are.

Of course, a good professional delivers good results.

Consistently.

But it's not always so.

So I'll ask you, do you want the result or the intention?

The ends or the means?

Is it better to be lucky than good?

items.title: Homeland
items.summary: When you look to the horizon, what do you want to…
items.content_html:
When you look to the horizon, what do you want to see?

Are you a mountain person?

⛰

Oceans or lakes?

🌊

Fields?

🏑

Woods?

🌲

Skyscrapers?

🏬

Sand?

🏜

Suburbia?

🏡

What makes you feel like you can exhale and breath in deeply?

What feels like home?

And what feels like just a nice place to visit?

And can I really, and I mean really, make a musings column only asking questions?

No.

items.title: Sandwich
items.summary: Alright. It's time to talk about punches. Now, there are a…
items.content_html:
Alright. It's time to talk about punches.

Now, there are a couple of ways to go about it.

But today I want to talk about knuckles.

Are you a two knuckle (index, middle) or a three knuckle (middle, ring, pinky) puncher?

I was always taught to hit with the big two, but always landed three knuckle.

Just felt more natural.

You want to make sure your knuckles are aligned with your wrist, so that your fist has the support of the entire forearm upon impact. A bent or misaligned fist is no fun.

And it's a snapping motion. 

Relax, relax, whip, snap, retract.

Like a snake.

Do not clench hard when starting the punch. 

Contract at impact (give or take).

Relax.

Step. Snap. Step back.

Step. Snap snap. Step back.

Step. Snap snap snap. Step back.

Good.

If your knuckles are scratched, it's because you're scraping down on the target.

Pop 'em.

In and out.

Jabs and crosses are straight lines.

Hooks and uppercuts are circles.

Everything starts and ends at your feet.

Center and turn at the waist.

The knuckles are just the follow-through of shoulders.

The inevitable.

Snap.

Now come back.

Relax.

Good.

👊🥪

items.title: All My Life
items.summary: Hello there. Today, I'd like to talk about love. When I…
items.content_html:
Hello there.

Today, I'd like to talk about love.

When I was dying, or rather being poisoned to kill the thing trying to kill me before either killed me, I remember driving.

Up and down.

An hour each way.

Over and over.

To the poison centers that were trying to heal me.

And I remember, during one of these times, listening to a movie.

I didn't watch, just listened.

And it was about a fit Asian man.

Who loved to cook.

Who used embarrassing bravery to meet the girl of his dreams.

And say hello.

They fell in love.

And he got sick.

And, it didn't end well for him.

But she was so strong.

And I remember listening and thinking, "Is this how my story ends?"

And I remember, getting so angry.

So angry.

Fuq dat.

And it helped me. 

A lot this movie.

We are given 27,375 days.

Give or take.

If we are lucky.

I am lucky.

Either way.

🎵 "That first night I was standing at your door." 🎶

And how, in our home at least, and maybe yours too, every story is a love story.

And this is mine.

🎵 "Yeah, I see us in black and white

Crystal clear on a star lit night

In all your gorgeous colors

I promise that I'll love you for the rest of my life." 🎶

Sometimes, you don't want a Hollywood ending.

Because you never want it to end.

To my beautiful wife:

For giving me everything to ever fight for.

For being so strong.

For our story.

I promise.

🍌

items.title: Gold Magic
items.summary: If you were a song, what song would you be? I…
items.content_html:
If you were a song, what song would you be?

I mean, what's your anthem?

Your theme song?

Your entrance song?

Your exit song?

"When it comes your time to die,

be not like those whose hearts are filled with the fear of death,

so that when their time comes they weep and pray

for a little more time to live their lives

over again in a different way.

Sing your death song

and die like a hero going home."

I mean, we got Sirius, from Alan Parsons.

Something by Tool.

Something by Pac.

Space Lord by Monster Magnet.

The clean version of the song's chorus goes "Space Lord, Mother, Mother."

Which I thought quirky and unique.

Only later did I realize that the original version had a crass word, and they used "Mother" twice instead of "Mother Bleeper."

But you know what?

I like Mother, Mother better.

Space Lord Mother Mother.

So I'd use the clean version.

My wife says the song's dirty.

Even clean.

And I suppose I am, too.

Dirty even clean.

🎵 "Take a sip from my secret potion,

I'll make you fall in love." 🎶

Hey!!!

We should have songs that define us.

Songs that just make us happy.

When we enter a room.

And when we leave it.

And everything in between.

🎵 "I could write it better than you ever felt it." 🎶

Hallelujah.

🎵 "I'm coming out the other side stronger.

One foot in front of the other.

Hands still raising.

Heart still praising." 🎶

Hallelujah, mother mothers.

items.title: I Was Inverted
items.summary: Mental models are an interesting thing. Basically, it's like a mini-rulebook…
items.content_html:
Mental models are an interesting thing.

Basically, it's like a mini-rulebook on how to think and therefore how to act.

One of note is to invert.

Always invert.

Made famous by Charlie Munger (RIP), you basically start at the end result of what you do not want and work backwards.

Want a good life?

How do you guarantee that you will live a bad one?

Then, do everything you can to avoid the things you listed as living a bad life.

Poor Charlie used to always say, "Where will I pass?"

And then joke that he just would never go to that location, and thus, live forever.

It's an interesting way to think.

Want to be fit?

How do you get fat?

Want to be rich?

How do you stay poor?

Want to be successful?

How do you lose at life?

Sometimes, narrowing it down helps.

What to do based on what not to do.

Next time you're stuck, think about it from a different point of view.

Upside down.

Decide what you do not want.

And give it the finger.

✈️

🛩

items.title: Late Again
items.summary: A great actor playing a terrible role once told another actor…
items.content_html:
A great actor playing a terrible role once told another actor on screen to "never go full absurd."

He said another word, but that's how I took it.

Never go full absurd.

That's the mistake.

Going maximal absurdity.

Leave a little room for normalcy.

Full absurd makes people squirmy.

Now, I typically enjoy that, but only in degrees.

And thus, believe it or not, I have to dial it down at times.

For the public.

For the column.

In pro wrestling, they say "just be yourself, dialed up to 11."

And in the modern day circus, this advice works.

But I'm just a dude in a spaceship, rocketing around the multiverse.

If I dialed it up to 11, I'd be in on Uranus.

🛸

items.title: Grand
items.summary: Workshop with me. Say I get to 60. What will I…
items.content_html:
Workshop with me.

Say I get to 60.

What will I be?

Will I be grand?

I hope to be grand.

But not in the way you might think (too soon!).

A mentor.

A founder.

Alive.

The last part is somewhat out of my control.

But I think I can do something about the other two.

I can mentor.

I can found.

I've done both.

I do both.

What does it mean to be a grandmentor?

What does it mean to be a grandfounder?

Mentor of mentors?

Founder of founders?

The cool thing about coining a term is you get to define what it means.

These are the types of things I enjoy doing.

Evoking with words.

Dreaming big.

Playing the long game.

items.title: Asymmetric Bettor
items.summary: I don't gamble. At least, not like most. I like little…
items.content_html:
I don't gamble.

At least, not like most.

I like little ventures, large outputs.

Now, you're thinking monetarily.

And that's where most people want to take it.

But if you think about it, little ins and big outs are what life is about.

Like a prism.

Light in, rainbow out.

This musings column, I'd like to think, is an asymmetric bet of sorts.

I just sit here and type.

A few minutes a day.

And let me tell you, it has been a huge win.

The trick then is to find your own arenas where a little goes a long way.

Writing.

Coding.

Hustling.

Love.

"You know, sometimes all you need is twenty seconds of insane courage, just literally, twenty seconds of just embarrassing bravery. And I promise you, something great will come of it."

To underdogs.

And underdawgs.

Betting on you.

🐾

items.title: Vidi, Vici, Veni
items.summary: You know, I hear they cancelled Latin. I do like a…
items.content_html:
You know, I hear they cancelled Latin.

I do like a lot of Latin, though.

It's an interesting language with lots of interesting words and phrases.

But we're not a language class.

We're here to...

[Covers mic.]

What are we here to do, again?

People just come here to read the musings?

No, no, that can't be right.

[Uncovers mic.]

We are here to talk about grace.

About giving it, receiving it, and how it took me ages to understand its power.

For many decades, I never gave it.

I was cold.

Unforgiving.

Ungraceful.

And it worked both ways.

I never gave myself any grace.

Nor did I give it to others.

Unless I didn't care about you. Then you're just invisible.

Ain't that something?

Not giving you grace meant I cared.

Back then.

I'm changed now, right?

Right??

Bah.

Now, respect I did give.

I was raised to respect.

"You're supposed to be my lab partner."

"I am!"

"No, you're not. You're disloyal."

And it's something I did give and did expect.

And if I didn't receive it, well, then there might be a problem.

But I've learned, as I approach these golden years, to extend grace in the face of disrespect.

At least, I know it on paper.

I'm not batting 1.000 here on the extend grace in the face of disrespect stat line.

But we all gotta start somewhere.

Grace falls from the sky like manna from Heaven.

I've lived many ways.

No grace.

Grace.

Everything in between.

Living on grace is a good way to be.

Respectfully,

𖤂
